status: SKIPPED — Spenser flagged scene as missing some links; broken on disk. Architectural pattern (R52) documented from graph probe only.
skip_reason: Spenser quote: 'hold up its missing some links you can skip the pyro one'
purpose: Architectural pattern of composing R49 (Voxelizer) with Pyro simulation as input. Animated Lego bricks following fire/smoke. Scene-as-built is broken; pattern documented for recipe library.
architecture_observed: Same 22-node Voxelizer graph as scene 15 Tutorial. Input pipeline: Pyro Output → Volume Loaders (density + color) → Volume Builder + Volume Mesher → fed into Voxelizer (R49) host's Connect children.
key_insight: R49 (Voxelizer) is INPUT-SOURCE-AGNOSTIC. Same graph works on static meshes, animated meshes, sim outputs, etc. The Pyro variant adds NO modifications inside the Voxelizer graph — pure recipe composition.

minimal_reproducible_subgraph.name: pyro_to_voxelizer_pipeline
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.purpose: Compose R49 (Voxelizer) with Pyro simulation. Animated voxel-block fire/smoke render.
minimal_reproducible_subgraph.value_proposition: Volume sim → block-grid render. Generalizes to: cloud sim → voxel clouds, water → voxel droplets, smoke → voxel haze, etc.

lessons_for_cinema4d_mcp: Recipe composition: R49 + Pyro = R52. No R49 modification needed.
Volume Loaders are the per-frame snapshot primitive for cached-sim-state-as-input.
Voxelizer is universal 'discretize any mesh' — recipes should chain it after any mesh-output source.

frames: frames/default.png — Pyro not cached, empty viewport
